title: SOC 2 CC4 — Fraud Risk Scenario Testing Evidence from Anomaly Correlation

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → 'CC7.2'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=security sourcetype="purple_team:event" earliest=-180d
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen, values(result) as results by scenario_id, phase
| join type=left scenario_id [
    search `notable` earliest=-180d tag=fraud_scenario
    | stats count as correlated_notables by rule_name
    | rename rule_name as scenario_id
  ]
| eval evidence_ok=if(isnotnull(correlated_notables) AND correlated_notables>0,1,0)
| where evidence_ok=0 AND phase="detection_validation"
| table scenario_id, phase, first_seen, last_seen, correlated_notables
value: CC4 includes fraud risk. Documenting that specific fraud scenarios (payroll diversion, vendor master change, refund abuse) are exercised and that monitoring fired in tabletop or purple-team events satisfies assessors that fraud is in scope.
implementation: (1) Maintain `scenario_id` alignment between purple team and ES rules; (2) tag notables used for fraud drills; (3) store facilitator sign-off outside Splunk, link `scenario_id`; (4) rerun after control changes; (5) red-team sensitive details in dashboards.
visualization: Table (gaps), Timeline (phases), Single value (scenarios without detection).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
